title: Variables: Storing Information in Your Programs
learningObjectives: Create variables to store different types of data
Understand how Python manages memory for variables
Use variables in calculations and operations
Watch variables change using the debugger

questions.question:
What will happen when this code runs?
```python
x = 10
x = x + 5
print(x)
```
options: Error - you can't assign x to itself
questions.explanation: The variable x starts with value 10, then gets updated to 10 + 5 = 15.

questions.question: Which variable name is NOT valid in Python?
questions.explanation: Variable names cannot start with a number. They must start with a letter or underscore.

questions.question: What type of data is stored in the variable `price = 19.99`?
questions.explanation: Numbers with decimal points are automatically recognized as float (floating-point) numbers.

codeExercises.title: Personal Information
codeExercises.description: Create variables to store information about yourself
codeExercises.starterCode:
def my_info():
    # TODO: Create these variables with your information
    first_name = "YourName"         # Replace with your first name
    last_name = "YourLastName"      # Replace with your last name
    birth_year = 2000               # Replace with your birth year
    favorite_color = "blue"         # Replace with your favorite color
    
    # Calculate your age (assuming current year is 2024)
    current_age = 2024 - birth_year
    
    # Create a full name
    full_name = first_name + " " + last_name
    
    print("Name:", full_name)
    print("Age:", current_age)
    print("Favorite color:", favorite_color)
codeExercises.expectedOutput:
Name: YourName YourLastName
Age: 24
Favorite color: blue
hints: Replace the placeholder values with your actual information
Make sure to use quotes around text values
